Its public compute footprint is execution-and-colocation shaped, NOT a megawatt research/ML campus: no self-built data center, no GPU/HPC cluster, and no MW figure appears anywhere in the public record. IMPORTANT DISAMBIGUATION: 'AQ Compute' is a separate European data-center operator (Aquila Group; 80% acquired by Bain Capital) and is UNRELATED to AQR Capital Management — do not map its Oslo/Barcelona sites to AQR. Lake Street, 50th Floor, Chicago, IL 60606 (firm locations page + OpenCorpData). ~18 global offices (NY 767 5th Ave; London 23 King St; plus Toronto, Warsaw, Hong Kong, Tokyo, Singapore, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, etc.) — offices only, no compute disclosed.

Compute footprint is CLOUD-NATIVE, two vendors, both consumed as services:
1) AWS — research/HPC. Primary source: AWS HPC blog case study describing AWS Batch + Amazon EKS, GPU-optimized instances, FSx for Lustre/S3/NFS, 100k+ jobs/week for ~160 investment teams. No AWS region or facility named.
2) OpenAI — AI research engine (BAM ChatGPT, BAM Embeddings, GPT-5.4 reasoning engine). Confirmed by both firm's own news page and OpenAI's customer story.

Additional signal (not mapped, low-value): press notes BAM has hired Google/AI talent to improve its AI stack (efinancialcareers), suggesting possible multi-model/multi-cloud, but no GCP/Azure facility or region is attributable to BAM in the public record.

NO physical data-center pin is justified: no self-build, no colocation announcement, no PeeringDB facility record found, no planning/permit or grid/EIA filing, no MW figure anywhere. Per project rules this is a cloud-only firm -> symbolic cloud_region site(s) at HQ. Coordinates for both records are SYMBOLIC at the Chicago HQ block (~41.8858, -87.6389) and must not be read as an actual compute location.

Caveat on execution/HFT: BAM runs quant/HFT-style pods, so it almost certainly leases exchange-colocation rack space via prime brokers/execution vendors at Equinix NY4/NY5 (Secaucus) / NY11 (Carteret) etc., but NO public source names a BAM-attributed execution colocation cage. Not mapped (would be inference below the evidence bar).

results.reasoning: Triple-checked. All four researcher evidence URLs were fetched and are LIVE and on-topic: two AWS engineering blogs co-authored by Bridgewater (Big Data 2022; Storage 2025), the Wiz customer case study, and Bridgewater's own careers/technology page. Every load-bearing specific survives verification: ~1,600 EC2 instances / 25,000 cores at peak, ~3,000 jobs/day, EMR-on-EKS single-Region-multi-AZ, multi-AWS-Region S3 replication, AWS+Azure with AKS+EKS across ~200 cloud accounts, hybrid-cloud framing. Critically, NO source names a specific AWS or Azure region — correctly reported as undisclosed. No self-built DC, colocation hall, or named cloud region exists in the public record. power_mw is correctly null (no MW figure anywhere). The disambiguation is sound and I re-confirmed it: 'Bridgewater' data-center listings are Bridgewater Township, NJ, not the fund. HQ address (One Nyala Farms Road, Westport CT 06880) is independently confirmed by multiple sources. I retained coord_precision=exact because the full street address is unambiguous and placeable, but tightened coord_basis to make explicit the pin is SYMBOLIC (cloud compute lives in undisclosed regions) and that it sits within the Nyala Farms campus/Westport. Confidence 'confirmed' is upheld for the cloud-first fact — backed by primary sources (firm's own site + two AWS engineering blogs co-authored by Bridgewater + Wiz). One record survives; two disambiguation false-positives dropped. Result is fully consistent with the essay's thesis: systematic-macro CPU/Spark workloads in public cloud, not a GPU-megawatts campus.
results.research_notes: Bridgewater Associates (Westport, CT systematic-macro fund) has NO publicly documented physical compute site — no self-built data centre, no colocation hall, no named AWS/Azure region. Its compute is cloud-first/hybrid multi-cloud on AWS (EKS) and Azure (AKS): mission-critical systems in the cloud, ~200 cloud accounts, peak ~1,600 EC2 instances / 25,000 cores for Spark research simulations, and multi-AWS-Region S3 replication. Consistent with the brief's prediction: systematic macro, CPU/Spark workloads, not a GPU-megawatts story. Per project rules for cloud-only-with-no-named-facility, I mapped ONE symbolic cloud_region pin anchored at the documented HQ (One Nyala Farms Road, Westport, CT 06880; coord from street address). power_mw is null (no source states MW; workloads run in AWS/Azure regions Bridgewater does not disclose). CPU-core figure is a peak AWS EKS metric from AWS's own blog, not a standing on-prem cluster. DISAMBIGUATION CAUTION: 'Bridgewater, New Jersey' is a township full of unrelated data centres (365 Data Centers NJ1, NYI NJ1 at 999 Frontier Road, etc.); those DataCenterMap/Baxtel/datacenters.com hits are the NJ town, NOT Bridgewater Associates the fund — do not map them to this firm. PeeringDB has no Bridgewater Associates ASN/facility (only Bridgewater State University, unrelated). DC Byte lists Bridgewater Associates only as a stub company profile with no facilities. No planning/permit/grid-connection filing for a Bridgewater-owned compute site was found. Confidence 'confirmed' applies to the cloud-first fact (firm site + AWS + Wiz primary sources); the map pin itself is explicitly symbolic, not a physical DC location.

sites.capacity_note: Proprietary self-built AI supercomputer 'WALLACE', unveiled Sept 2023 at the Toronto HQ. Claimed 'quadrillions of floating-point calculations per second' (i.e. 2+ petaflops per DCD). Custom-designed hardware, chassis, cooling and software. Fluorinert liquid-cooled system, ~7.5 ft x 5.5 ft footprint; unit ~2m tall x 1.5m wide. Reported build cost ~US$10M (DCD, citing Financial News). Firm's own description references allocation of tasks between CPU and GPU cores, but no GPU vendor, chip model, core count, or power (MW/kW) figure is disclosed publicly. No colocation, cloud region, or named third-party data center identified in any source.

results.reasoning: All five evidence URLs were fetched and verified live and on-point (LinkedIn returned the stated 161 Bay St address; the two GlobeNewswire releases, DCD, and Hedgeweek all support the Toronto on-prem WALLACE claim). The firm's own castleridgemgt.com contact/home pages returned HTTP 403 and are excluded from evidence, but they are not needed. One compute site survives: the self-built on-prem 'WALLACE' AI supercomputer at Castle Ridge's Toronto HQ (unveiled Sept 2023), an HQ HPC room — not a colocation hall or cloud region. The firm designed and manufactured the machine and its fluorinert liquid-cooling in-house. No public record ties WALLACE to any third-party colocation provider, cloud region, or named data center; no GPU vendor/chip model or power (MW) figure is disclosed, so gpu_type, gpu_count, cpu_cores, and power_mw are all null (absence is the finding). The New York office (mentioned in press and LinkedIn) is an office only with no compute in any source, so it is correctly NOT listed as a compute site. Coordinate correction: the researcher's lon of -79.3812 sat ~100m west of 161 Bay Street; nudged to 43.6472, -79.3798 to match TD Canada Trust Tower (Brookfield Place) more precisely, kept at approximate precision since no source states coordinates and the street address comes from LinkedIn rather than a WALLACE-specific source. Confidence 'confirmed' is justified: existence and Toronto-HQ location rest on the firm's own corporate press releases plus three independent trade-press corroborations (DCD, Hedgeweek, GlobeNewswire/Morningstar syndication); only the exact street address is softer, which is reflected in approximate coord precision rather than site confidence. No specifics were stripped — the $10M cost, fluorinert cooling, footprint, and 2+ petaflops all survive as capacity_note descriptors backed by DCD/press; none were misrepresented as power_mw.
results.research_notes: Single compute site found: the self-built on-prem 'WALLACE' AI supercomputer at Castle Ridge's Toronto headquarters (unveiled Sept 2023). It is an HQ HPC room, not a colocation hall or cloud region — the firm designed and built the machine and its fluorinert liquid-cooling in-house specifically because, per the firm, 'traditional AI hardware was not designed for the way [their Geno-Synthetic Algorithms] work.' No public record ties WALLACE to any third-party colocation provider, cloud region, or named data center; no GPU vendor/chip model or power (MW) figure has been disclosed, so gpu_type/gpu_count/power_mw are left null (absence is the finding). The New York office (340 Madison Ave, per LinkedIn) is an office only with no compute mentioned in any source, so it is NOT listed as a compute site. HQ street address has a conflict in third-party directories: the firm's own LinkedIn gives 161 Bay Street Suite 2700 (TD Canada Trust Tower), while ProfileCanada lists an older 100 King St W Suite 4710 — WALLACE-specific press sources only say 'headquarters in Toronto,' so coord_precision is kept approximate. Confidence graded 'confirmed' because the existence and Toronto-HQ location of WALLACE rest on the firm's own corporate press releases plus independent DCD trade-press corroboration; only the exact street address is softer.

results.reasoning: Adversarial verification of the single claimed site (CTC HQ at 425 S Financial Place / Gateway Plaza, Chicago). I fetched all four evidence URLs; all are live and support the location. The firm's own /about page states "425 S. Financial Place, 4th Floor, Chicago, IL 60605" and describes a "former trading floor." The CIM Group landlord press release independently confirms the building (Gateway Plaza, 39-story, ~1.04M sq ft), the 114,000 sq ft footprint, and floors 4/5/6. The LEI registry corroborates the HQ street address (caveat: the LEI entity is "Chicago Trading Company (CTC) Limited" with a Hong Kong legal address — the international entity — but its stated HQ address matches, so it still corroborates the location). The FPGA careers page confirms in-house low-latency/FPGA trading-system engineering at the HQ and names no external facility. Confidence stays "confirmed" — the strongest sources are primary (firm site + landlord press release). Coordinates independently verified via search: 425 S Financial Place sits at ~41.876, -87.632, placing the researcher's 41.8757/-87.633 squarely on the building; coord_precision=exact is justified by the source-stated full street address. No power_mw, GPU, or CPU figures are stated anywhere, so all remain null. Independent searches ("Chicago Trading Company" + data center / colocation / Cermak / Aurora / Equinix) returned only generic financial-colocation hubs; no public source names CTC at any specific hall, cloud region, or HPC room. Per project rules those inferred colo sites are correctly NOT included. No sites dropped; the HQ record survives fully. The absence of a mappable compute facility is itself the finding. Note: I could not personally fetch the Business Wire copy of the CIM press release (connection timed out), so I did not add it to evidence, but three independent live sources already confirm the address.

results.reasoning: All three original evidence URLs are live and support their claims; I fetched each. Hedgeweek confirms Citadel the hedge fund actively runs/expands in-house compute but gives no location. Google Cloud is explicitly Citadel Securities (market maker) — a distinct entity — and states no DC location, so it supports disambiguation only, not a hedge-fund compute site. Baxtel confirms the Switch 'Citadel Campus' false positive. I added The Real Deal (830 Brickell office lease) as a credible-press anchor for the Miami HQ pin. Independent searches surfaced NO named physical compute site for the hedge fund: no self-build DC, no colo hall, no disclosed cloud region, no grid/EIA/interconnection or planning filing. The arXiv microbenchmarking papers show a 'Citadel' Chicago-OFFICE affiliation and state no data-center location. Corrections applied: (1) nulled gpu_type — the microbenchmarking papers do not establish a hardware footprint at any mapped location, so 'NVIDIA A100/Volta/Turing' over-specified a symbolic pin; the nuance stays in capacity_note. (2) Confidence held at 'inferred' (no primary source places compute anywhere). (3) Coordinates held symbolic at the Miami HQ (25.7657,-80.1936 sits in downtown Miami/Brickell — plausible). Result: one honest symbolic 'compute location undisclosed' marker survives; absence of a mappable physical footprint is itself the finding. Nothing dropped from the surviving set; four false positives/wrong-entity items recorded as dropped for the record.
results.research_notes: DISAMBIGUATION IS THE CENTRAL ISSUE for this firm. (1) Citadel LLC (hedge fund) vs Citadel Securities (market maker) are separate entities that historically shared HPC talent — Daniele Scarpazza led the 'High Performance Computing Team at Citadel LLC' from Nov 2014, then moved to a Citadel Securities R&D role in Jan 2022 (LinkedIn/Google Scholar); the bare-metal NVIDIA GPU microbenchmarking papers (arXiv 1804.06826 Volta, 1903.07486 Turing, plus Graphcore IPU report) are branded 'Citadel'/'Citadel Securities' with no physical DC location stated. A Chicago office address (131 S. Dearborn St.) appears on some HPC-team academic affiliations, and there is a public 'Global Data Center Manager - Citadel LLC' LinkedIn profile — location signals for offices, NOT sourced data-center sites. (2) FALSE POSITIVES excluded: Switch's 'Citadel Campus' / Tahoe Reno (operator = Switch Inc., not the fund); 'Citadel Data Centers' Austin TX (unrelated colocation aggregator, citadeldc.com); 'Citadel BESS LLC' in EIA (battery storage developer, unrelated); GDS International Series B backing by Ken Griffin (a ~$1.2bn investment, not Citadel's own compute). (3) HQ facts: Citadel moved HQ from Chicago to Miami in 2022; currently at 830 Brickell Plaza; future 54-story tower planned at 1201 Brickell Bay Drive (Commercial Observer / CoStar) — these are OFFICES, not data centers. (4) Griffin is publicly skeptical that generative AI yet generates hedge-fund alpha (Hedgeweek, Oct 2025), even as Citadel expands compute. If the map wants ZERO speculative pins, this record could instead be recorded as found=false with the same null_reason — I returned one symbolic HQ marker because the firm verifiably operates compute (just at an undisclosed location) rather than being execution-only. Recommend labeling it clearly on the map as 'compute location undisclosed'. Several key trade sources (efinancialcareers.com, DCD) returned 405/403 to automated fetch; their findings here are corroborated via search snippets and independent sources.

results.reasoning:
Adversarial verification of all four researcher sites; every evidence URL was fetched. Outcome: all four sites SURVIVE, none dropped, but confidence/specifics tightened and one dead source (HPCwire, persistent HTTP 403) replaced.

SITE 1 — Piscataway (Digital Realty colo suite fit-out): SURVIVES, graded 'reported'. Contractor page (Dimensional Design Systems) verified — states "During the summer of 2020, D.E. Shaw & Co. had begun expanding their data center suite campus in Piscataway, New Jersey." datacenterHawk (403 on first try, resolved on retry) and Highland Associates both verified: operator = Digital Realty, 3 Corporate Place / 1115 Centennial Ave, Piscataway NJ. Highland confirms campus scale (135,000 sq ft, 26 MW utility capacity, 69kV/80MVA substation) but explicitly does NOT name D.E. Shaw — this is campus context only, NOT D.E. Shaw's allocation. Only one source (contractor) ties D.E. Shaw to a specific suite, so 'reported' is correct. power_mw kept null (26 MW is the Digital Realty building, not the fund's lease). coord_precision=city_level correct (specific building of D.E. Shaw suite not disclosed).

SITE 2 — NYC HQ (Two Manhattan West): SURVIVES, 'confirmed'. deshaw.com/contact and /what-we-do both verified the HQ address "Two Manhattan West, 375 Ninth Avenue, New York, NY 10001" and the 750-developer/technology framing. PeeringDB AS4195 verified: org "D. E. Shaw & Co.", policy Restrictive, no facilities/IX listed — supports the deliberate-opacity finding. NOTE: /what-we-do does not actually mention a "Systems group" or "state-of-the-art data centers" (I could not verify that phrasing on the fetched page); softened capacity_note to what the pages actually state. coord_precision=exact justified by source-stated street address (Manhattan West, 9th Ave at 31st/33rd; lat/lon plausible).

SITE 3 — DESRES NYC HPC (Anton, 120 W. 45th St.): SURVIVES, 'confirmed'. deshawresearch.com/technology.html verified the address "120 W. 45th St. New York, NY 10036" and that DESRES operates Anton systems "for in-house research and development ... drug discovery." PSC page confirms DESRES designs/builds Anton (distinct from the fund). STRIPPED unverified specifics: the "Floor 39", "site of first Anton machine", and "Anton 1 operational Oct 2008, 512 nodes" details are NOT on either fetched evidence URL (only corroborated by a web search, not a fetched supporting source), so I removed them from coord_basis/capacity_note. Address is source-stated, so coord_precision=exact stands.

SITE 4 — Anton 3 at PSC (Pittsburgh): SURVIVES, 'confirmed'. psc.edu/resources/anton verified: "A 64-node, third-generation Anton system became operational at PSC on April 1, 2025 ... designed and constructed by D. E. Shaw Research (DESRES) ... without cost ... non-commercial research." The researcher's HPCwire evidence URL is DEAD for verification purposes (persistent HTTP 403 on multiple attempts — WebFetch cannot read hpcwire.com), so I DROPPED that evidence item and REPLACED it with two independently-fetched sources: DataCenterDynamics ("Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center (PSC) has started operating its Anton 3 ... Developed by D. E. Shaw Research") and Carnegie Mellon University ("Designed and built by D. E. Shaw Research ... will ... arrive at the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center"). CMU also confirms PSC is a joint CMU/Pitt center in Pittsburgh. coord_precision=city_level correct (specific PSC data-hall address not source-stated).

CROSS-CUTTING: The critical scope distinction is sound and verified — D.E. Shaw & Co. (the hedge fund / mapping target) vs. D.E. Shaw Research/DESRES (a separate molecular-dynamics lab whose Anton supercomputers do biochemistry, not trading). Sites 3 and 4 are DESRES research compute, retained with explicit "not the trading firm" caveats. The honest public-record finding for the trading firm is deliberate opacity: only one documented trading-side compute site (Piscataway colo, 'reported'); AS4195 is restrictive with zero listed facilities. No MW attributable to any D.E. Shaw-operated compute anywhere; power_mw null throughout. D.E. Shaw Renewable Investments (DESRI) solar projects correctly excluded (energy investment, not the firm's own compute). No site dropped entirely; the only removal was one unverifiable HPCwire URL, replaced by two working sources.

results.research_notes:
CRITICAL SCOPE NOTE: Two distinct David-Shaw entities must not be conflated. (1) D.E. Shaw & Co. = the hedge fund / quant firm (the mapping target). (2) D.E. Shaw Research (DESRES) = a separate molecular-dynamics / drug-discovery lab that builds the Anton special-purpose supercomputers. The famous 'D.E. Shaw supercomputers' (Anton) do biochemistry, NOT trading. The task itself flags this. Many GPU/HPC/'custom supercomputer' job ads and press attributed to 'D.E. Shaw' are DESRES, not the fund.

TRADING FIRM (D.E. Shaw & Co.) footprint - the honest public-record finding is DELIBERATE OPACITY: its own site says the Systems group runs 'state-of-the-art data centers' but names none; its ASN AS4195 is a restrictive, private-only PeeringDB record with ZERO listed facilities/IX. The ONLY publicly documented trading-side compute site is a colocation suite fit-out at Digital Realty's Piscataway, NJ campus (summer 2020), disclosed by the electrical-BIM contractor Dimensional Design Systems - graded 'reported' (contractor page; no independent second confirmation of D.E. Shaw's specific building/suite, and no MW attributable to the fund's portion). Like most HFT/quant firms it also almost certainly colocates at the NJ exchange venues (Mahwah/Carteret/Secaucus) but NO public source names a specific D.E. Shaw hall there, so none is mapped.

RESEARCH (DESRES) footprint is far better documented: NYC HPC facility at 120 W. 45th St. (Anton 1 operational Oct 2008; runs in-house Anton machines) and the DESRES-built, NIH-funded, PSC-HOSTED Anton 3 in Pittsburgh (operational 1 Apr 2025). Both included with explicit 'not trading / not the fund' caveats so a reader isn't misled.

OUT OF SCOPE / excluded to avoid error: D.E. Shaw Renewable Investments (DESRI) develops SOLAR projects (e.g. 180 MW Cove Mountain, Utah) that power FACEBOOK data centers - this is an energy-investment business, not D.E. Shaw's own compute, so it is NOT a compute site. No MW figure is stated anywhere for any D.E. Shaw-operated compute; power_mw left null throughout (the 26 MW / 80 MVA Piscataway figures are the Digital Realty building/campus, not D.E. Shaw's lease). Coordinates: two exact street addresses (both NYC HQs), Piscataway and PSC left city_level because the specific building within each campus is not source-stated.

sites.capacity_note: Strike Technologies Services LLC, an affiliate of Global Trading Systems LLC (GTS), built StrikeNET, an ultra-low-latency microwave network linking the CME data center in Aurora, IL and the NASDAQ data center in Carteret, NJ (record ~4.17 ms rack-to-rack, >99.7% availability). GTS agreed to sell the network to TMX Group in Aug 2014; the acquisition completed Oct 31, 2014 (operated thereafter by TMX Atrium) — so this is no longer GTS-operated infrastructure. Recorded as a historical GTS Tier-2 (network) footprint. No MW/compute specs; it is relay/latency infrastructure, not a compute DC.

results.research_notes:
Target disambiguation: the subject is GTS / Global Trading Systems (gtsx.com), the NYC electronic market-maker and NYSE DMM run by Ari Rubenstein — NOT the several unrelated 'GTS' entities that dominate search (Global Technical Systems, a US defense HPC contractor at gts.us.com; GTS Telecom / GTS Poland / Global Telecommunication Systems ISPs; GTS-TS, gogtss.com and other IT-services firms). Those were excluded.

What the public record supports vs. does not:
- CONFIRMED offices: 625 Sixth Avenue / Avenue of the Americas (Chelsea HQ, ~35k SF, 3rd floor, leased 2024) and 40 Wall Street. Both are offices; GTS careers material references a 'fully-staffed data center operations team' and a multi-business production network with 'ultra-low-latency trading paths,' which strongly implies on-prem/managed trading kit — but NO named colocation hall, cloud region, GPU/ML cluster, or megawatt figure is anywhere in the public record for this firm.
- INFERRED execution: as NYSE DMM (acquired Barclays' DMM business, 2016; 1,200+ listed securities), GTS necessarily runs execution at the NYSE Mahwah data center, but no source names a GTS rack/cage/power there. Similarly, a US ~3-5%-of-cash-equities market-maker would sit in Equinix NY4/NY5 (Secaucus) and Nasdaq Carteret (NY11), but I found NO source that explicitly places GTS in those halls, so I did not create pins for them (project rule: no un-sourced location).
- HISTORICAL: StrikeNet microwave (CME Aurora IL <-> NASDAQ Carteret NJ), built by GTS subsidiary Strike Technologies, was SOLD to TMX Group in Aug 2014 — recorded as historical, confidence 'reported'.

Coordinates: HQ and 40 Wall Street are exact (documented street addresses). Mahwah coord is the NYSE facility address (1700 MacArthur Blvd) but GTS's specific footprint within it is inferred, not source-stated. StrikeNet is city-level (Aurora endpoint shown).

No MW figures exist in the public record for any GTS site — no grid/EIA/permit filing surfaced; power_mw null everywhere. No ASN for the trading firm was matchable on PeeringDB/bgp.he.net (the 'GTS' ASNs found are unrelated telecoms). Bottom line: GTS is essentially an execution/market-making firm whose live compute is on-prem-office + undisclosed exchange colocation; the ML-megawatt thesis does not apply here — this is a microseconds firm, not a megawatts one, and the absence of any disclosed compute campus is itself the finding.

results.reasoning: no sites: Execution-only proprietary trading firm with no public compute footprint. Hard Eight Trading, LLC (founded 2004) is a small Chicago-based prop options/futures market-maker that, per its own site, trades "across all major asset classes on over 20 global exchanges using our own capital" — it serves no external clients and does no ML/compute research. Searches of the firm's own site, careers pages, press, DataCenterDynamics/DataCenterMap/Baxtel, colocation-provider case studies, and PeeringDB returned no named self-built data centre, colocation hall, cloud region, or GPU/HPC facility attributed to the firm. Its trading almost certainly runs on leased racks inside exchange colocation halls (e.g. CME Aurora, Cboe), but no public source names Hard Eight as a tenant, so nothing is mappable. The only public location is its office in the Chicago Board of Trade building at 141 W Jackson Blvd, Suite 1175, Chicago, IL — an execution/office presence, not a compute site with disclosed specs or MW. No power, GPU, or data-centre disclosures exist. Absence is the finding.

results.reasoning:
One surviving site: the Headlands / Arkansas Tech Systems LLC supercomputer data center (renovation of the former Acxiom DC) in west Little Rock, AR. This is a research/ML (HPC) self-build site, not execution colocation.

URL VERIFICATION: I fetched every evidence URL. Three fetched cleanly and support the record: (1) CBRE listing -- confirms '15900 Arkansas Systems Drive, Little Rock, AR 72223' and that the site is 'directly to the North of the former Acxiom Data Center'; (2) SimplyHired -- confirms Headlands Tech hiring a Data Center Technician in Little Rock, AR; (3) Polk Stanley Wilcox -- confirms the former Acxiom DC building specs. (4) The DCD 'mystery developer' article was fetched and confirms that project is Willowbend Capital's, NOT Headlands -- a useful disambiguation source, kept. The two City of Little Rock PDFs (the primary certification documents that establish the Headlands->Arkansas Tech Systems->supercomputer link, $120M, 5 jobs) could NOT be personally fetched: littlerock.gov repeatedly refused the connection (ECONNREFUSED at 74.84.144.25) across multiple attempts. These are genuinely live public documents -- they appear as current search results and their exact language ('considering purchasing a vacant end-of-life data center,' 'state-of-the-art supercomputer,' 'direct to chip liquid cooling,' '$120 million... new taxable capital investment,' 'five (5) new jobs') is quoted verbatim in fresh WebSearch snippets. I kept one of the two city PDFs in evidence but flagged in its 'supports' note that I could not personally fetch it, so it does not by itself justify a 'confirmed' grade.

CONFIDENCE re-graded from 'confirmed' to 'reported'. The strongest sources I personally verified (CBRE, SimplyHired, Polk Stanley Wilcox) anchor the location, the Headlands DC presence, and the building, but the firm->subsidiary->supercomputer->investment chain comes from the primary city documents I could not fetch (only corroborated via search). Multiple credible sources agree, so this is above pure inference, but strictly below 'confirmed' because the primary certification PDFs were not directly retrievable in this pass.

COORDINATES downgraded from 'exact' to 'approximate'. The researcher used 15900 Arkansas Systems Drive geocoded via Nominatim and called it building-level exact. But the CBRE source it relies on says that address is a development site NORTH of the Acxiom DC with 'exact address to be determined.' The actual Headlands facility is the former Acxiom building, whose precise street coordinate is not source-locked by any URL I could fetch. City/country (Little Rock / USA) are solid; lat/lon plausibly sit on the correct campus road in west Little Rock (72223), so I retained the coordinate but marked it approximate rather than exact.

STRIPPED SPECIFICS: power_mw stays null (no source states MW for the Headlands facility -- the 150 MW / 100+ MW / $6B / $21B / 1 GW figures belong to AVAIO 'Leo,' Google Port of Little Rock, and Willowbend, all separate projects). No GPU/CPU counts disclosed; source language is qualitative only. Status kept as 'planned' (city docs May 2025 say 'considering purchasing'; renovation not yet operational). No execution colocation is publicly attributed to Headlands, and no named HPC room exists at any HQ office, so no additional pins were added -- absence of public evidence is the finding there.
results.research_notes:
Single publicly-documented compute site: a self-build/renovation research data center in west Little Rock, AR, run via Headlands' subsidiary Arkansas Tech Systems LLC. It is the firm's ML/HPC ('supercomputer') footprint, not execution colocation. Status = planned/early: the city documents (May 2025) describe the firm as 'considering purchasing' and seeking tax-back certification; the Acxiom-to-Arkansas Tech Systems sale (~$5.2M) is reported in Arkansas Business 'Real Deals,' and Headlands has since posted a Little Rock data-center technician role, indicating the project is proceeding.

IMPORTANT DISAMBIGUATION: Little Rock is a hot data-center market in 2025-26. Do NOT conflate Headlands with separate, larger nearby projects: (1) AVAIO Digital 'Leo' campus (~$6B initial / >$21B full build, 150 MW contracted with Entergy, up to ~1 GW) and (2) Google's Port of Little Rock data center (>100 MW). Those MW figures are NOT Headlands. A third project (DCD 'mystery developer,' Willowbend Capital, $1bn, 300,000 sq ft at the Port of Little Rock) is also unrelated.

No MW figure is stated anywhere in the public record for the Headlands facility, so power_mw is null (do not infer). No GPU count/type disclosed; source language is qualitative ('world's fastest computer hardware,' 'extremely dense server cabinets,' 'direct-to-chip liquid cooling,' 'battery storage').

Coordinates: 15900 Arkansas Systems Drive is a documented full street address (a short private campus road, formerly Acxiom / Arkansas Systems, part of the Chenal Technology Center land assembly). Nominatim placed the street at 34.7642, -92.4524 (west Little Rock, Big Rock Township, Pulaski County, 72223); treated as exact/building-level. The US Census geocoder did not match this private drive.

HQ / offices (context, not mapped as compute -- no public evidence of a named HPC room): Chicago HQ 110 N Wacker Dr, 54th Fl (60606); plus NY (575 5th Ave), Austin (111 Congress Ave), Amsterdam (WTC Tower 2, Strawinskylaan 1847), London (125 Wood St), Singapore (8 Marina View). Execution colocation (e.g. Equinix NY4, Nasdaq Carteret, CME/Aurora) is NOT publicly attributed to Headlands, so no execution colo pins were mapped -- absence of public evidence is the finding there.

results.reasoning:
I fetched all 11 evidence URLs across the three sites. Ten supported their claims; one (the wtc.com press-release URL for the 3 WTC footprint) did NOT contain the HRT footprint/floors content and was dropped and replaced with a verified Commercial Observer article. All three sites survive with a supporting primary source each.

SITE 1 (Lefdal, Norway): Fully corroborated. Dell newsroom (May 14, 2026) states verbatim "HRT recently opened a data center at Lefdal Mine Data Centers on Norway's west coast" and names Dell PowerEdge XE9685L (AMD EPYC), NVIDIA HGX B200, direct liquid cooling, Spectrum-X networking. HRT's own LinkedIn post confirms the Lefdal deployment and "direct-liquid-cooled racks of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs operating above 125 kW per cabinet." PeeringDB fac/7677 gives coords 61.934669, 5.112483. Operator site gives address Gate 1 nr 101, 6700 Maloy, up to 200 MW, 100% renewable, fjord cooling. Confidence stays CONFIRMED (multiple primary/operator sources). NOTE: PeeringDB street number ("Gt 1 nr 57") differs slightly from the operator page ("Gate 1 nr 101") — both in Maloy 6700; I reconciled the PeeringDB quote to what the page actually returned. power_mw kept null (200 MW is venue-wide, not HRT's share — correct per rules). coord_precision kept approximate: coords are the facility centroid; the specific HRT hall inside the mine is not disclosed and city is Maloy/Lefdal (Kinn municipality, Vestland). I kept the researcher's "Maloy / Lefdal (Nordfjord), Kinn municipality, Vestland" framing, which correctly flags both the postal municipality and the mine's location.

SITE 2 (NYC HQ, 3 WTC): HRT's own contact page and offices page both confirm the exact address "3 World Trade Center, 175 Greenwich Street, 76th Floor, New York, NY 10007." I DROPPED the wtc.com press-release URL as evidence because when fetched it did not name HRT, gave no square footage, and gave no floors — it fails the "must support the claim" test. I REPLACED it with the Commercial Observer article (credible press), which confirms 211,207 sq ft total at 3 WTC. NOTE: Commercial Observer states the occupied/expansion floors are 72 and 73, while HRT's own page lists the 76th floor — a genuine discrepancy; I kept HRT's own stated address (176th->76th floor) as the primary and did not assert a single floor number in capacity_note. coord_precision stays exact (unambiguous street address at a named skyscraper). Confidence stays CONFIRMED (firm's own site + credible press). This is an on-prem HQ / HPC-environment pin; no named on-site data hall or MW is public, correctly left null.

SITE 3 (Cloud — Google Cloud + Lambda, symbolic NYC): Google Cloud press corner (2024-07-18) confirms HRT uses Google Cloud HPC incl. NVIDIA GPUs, Dynamic Workload Scheduler, Spot/Preemptible VMs — no region named. Lambda vendor blog confirms NVIDIA HGX B200 systems, networking/storage/orchestration, and the on-prem-ceiling framing — no facility named. DCD independently corroborates the Lambda selection. All three URLs verified and support the claims. Symbolic cloud_region pin at HQ is correct because no provider discloses a region/facility. Confidence: the FACT that HRT uses these providers is backed by primary corporate sources, so CONFIRMED is defensible; the LOCATION is symbolic and coord_precision=symbolic reflects that honestly.

ABSENCE-IS-THE-FINDING (upheld): HRT plainly operates large undisclosed US on-prem GPU/CPU clusters (Lambda's own framing that on-prem was "reaching its ceiling"; Dell storage relationships), but NO named US self-build data-center city, permit, or grid filing exists in the public record. Correctly NOT mapped as a distinct pin. No fabricated MW anywhere. Stripped nothing further — the researcher had already kept all MW null and all undisclosed GPU/rack counts null.

results.reasoning:
Verified every claimed URL by fetching it. All three sites SURVIVE as on_prem_hq offices; none dropped. Corrections made:

CHICAGO HQ (kept, confirmed): All three cited URLs fetched and support the site. BUT narrowed the GlobeNewswire evidence 'supports' text - on fetch, the press release does NOT mention any servers/UPS/data infrastructure (only amenities like rooftop terrace, fitness center, LEED cert). It supports location + 38,000 sq ft + operational status only. The on-prem UPS/cooling tech-room claim rests solely on the contractor (BIG Construction) source, which does verify verbatim. Coords sit at 1 N Dearborn, Chicago Loop -> exact stands.

NEW YORK (kept, confirmed; evidence repaired): Researcher's primary cited URL (oldmissioncapital.com/2020/09/30/global-market-maker-old-mission-expands-at-120-broadway/) returned HTTP 404 - DEAD, removed. The GlobeSt URL returned only a headline with no extractable article body - too weak, removed. REPLACED with connectcre.com, which I fetched and which explicitly names 'Old Mission Capital' as a leased tenant at 120 Broadway (11,173 -> 20,023 sq ft). Combined with the firm's own contact-us page (verified: '120 Broadway Street, 11th Floor, New York, NY 10271'), the NY office is now backed by two surviving, fetched sources. Coords sit at 120 Broadway (Equitable Building), Financial District -> exact stands.

LONDON (kept, confirmed; evidence UPGRADED): Researcher had only ONE source (the firm contact-us page). I added two UK Companies House PRIMARY/regulator records (OC424683 Old Mission Global Trading LLP and OC403724 Old Mission Europe LLP, active), both fetched and both stating registered office '1 Finsbury Square, 4th Floor, London, England, EC2A 1AE'. This strengthens London from single-source to a regulator-backed, triple-checked site. Coords sit at Finsbury Square, London EC2A -> exact stands.

ABSENCE FINDINGS confirmed by targeted searches: NO public source names any specific exchange colocation facility/provider for Old Mission; NO PeeringDB/ASN record (AS54103 'MOD Mission Critical' is an unrelated Colorado host - name collision, correctly excluded); NO self-built data center, contractor DC announcement, grid/EIA/planning MW filing, or named cloud region. As a global market maker its execution infra is certainly colocated at exchange DCs, but since no site/provider is named publicly, no colocation pin is justifiable under project rules - absence is the finding for the execution, network, and research (self-build/cloud) tiers. All power_mw, GPU, and CPU fields remain null (nothing source-stated anywhere).
results.research_notes:
Old Mission (Old Mission Capital, LLC) is a self-funded ETF/multi-asset quantitative market maker and HFT firm, founded 2008, with three PUBLICLY DOCUMENTED offices: Chicago HQ (1 N. Dearborn, 8th Fl), New York (120 Broadway, 11th Fl), London (1 Finsbury Square, 4th Fl). All three addresses are firm-stated and cross-checked against press/real-estate sources.

COMPUTE FOOTPRINT - what the public record supports and what it does not:
- The ONLY documented physical compute/tech-room signal is the Chicago HQ build-out, where contractor BIG Construction states the 2021 trading office included a dedicated UPS room and supplemental cooling - a genuine on-prem trading tech / server room (not a purpose-built data center). No compute specs or MW disclosed anywhere.
- NY (120 Broadway) and London (1 Finsbury Square) are firm-stated trading offices; no dedicated server-room/DC capacity, compute specs, or MW are documented for either. Included as on_prem_hq offices for map completeness; compute at both is undisclosed.

ABSENCE FINDINGS (searched and confirmed nothing public):
- NO named exchange colocation facility. As a global market maker active across "virtually every exchange-listed asset class globally," Old Mission's execution infra is certainly colocated at exchange data centers (CME/Aurora, NYSE/Mahwah, Nasdaq/Carteret, Equinix Secaucus, London LD4, etc.) via job ads referencing a "low-latency trading network," but the firm never names any specific colocation site or provider in the public record - so no colocation pin can be justified under project rules.
- NO PeeringDB / ASN / network-facility record for Old Mission. (AS54103 "MOD Mission Critical" is an UNRELATED Colorado hosting company - name collision only; not Old Mission.)
- NO self-built data center, NO contractor DC announcement, NO grid-connection / EIA / planning filing leaking MW, NO named cloud region. Their quant research references "machine learning" and "high-performance distributed systems" but discloses no compute location, provider, GPU/CPU counts, or power.

Net: found=true with three firm-owned office sites (Chicago the only one with a documented on-prem tech room). No self-build DC, no named colo, no cloud region, no MW anywhere in the public record - absence is the finding for those tiers.

results.research_notes:
PDT Partners (spun out of Morgan Stanley's Process Driven Trading in 2013; CEO Peter Muller) discloses NO self-built data centre, NO named external colocation hall, and NO MW/power figure anywhere in the public record. Extensive targeted searches (DataCenterMap/Baxtel, PeeringDB/ASN, "megawatt/campus/build", colocation near NJ exchanges Mahwah/Carteret/Secaucus, grid/permit) returned nothing attributable to PDT.

What the public record DOES support: (1) PDT runs a self-operated on-prem HPC/research-computing environment (Linux clusters, HPC/Grid, CPU + GPU; schedulers Slurm/Torque/SGE) bridged to public cloud - confirmed by multiple PDT careers postings (Cloud Engineer, HPC Platform Engineer, Performance Engineer citing PyTorch/CUDA/GPU, Senior Network Engineer). The physical machine-room location of this on-prem gear is not named by any source; it is reasonably associated with the NYC HQ, hence HQ/city-level, confidence 'inferred'. (2) AWS is PDT's named public cloud (incl. AWS Batch / AWS ParallelCluster for research-computing burst); no AWS region, size, or MW disclosed - recorded as a symbolic cloud_region anchored at HQ.

Two confirmed office addresses (from PDT's own contact page): NYC 60 Columbus Circle 18th Fl (primary, where tech/HPC teams sit 3 days/week) and London 119-121 Cannon Street. Office building coordinates are 'exact' (geocode); the COMPUTE at those sites is not separately confirmed, so its confidence is 'inferred' even though building coords are precise.

Caveat per project rules: this is a case where the office/campus is known but the compute is inferred and its exact housing undisclosed - absence of a named DC/MW is itself the finding. No source should be read as confirming a discrete PDT-owned data centre. Wikipedia and aggregator lead-gen pages (ZoomInfo/RocketReach) were not used as evidence. Note the two office addresses could optionally be collapsed to one on-prem record if the map treats office nodes as a single tier; kept separate here to preserve both exact addresses.

results.reasoning:
Triple-checked Point72 / Cubist. Both researcher sites SURVIVE but with a dead URL swapped and confidence downgraded.

VERIFIED LIVE (fetched successfully, support their claims): (1) anyscale.com/blog/ray-day-nyc-recap — exact quote 'runs on-premise multi-tenant Ray clusters plus Anyscale in the cloud', speaker Todd Gaugler, Quant Developer, Cubist Systematic Strategies (Point72). No physical location stated. (2) point72.com/cubist/ — Cubist described as 'systematic, computer-driven trading strategies based on rigorous research'; no infrastructure/location. (3) point72.com/locations/ — HQ '72 Cummings Point Road, Stamford, CT 06902' confirmed.

DEAD URL removed: the researcher's greenhouse.io job URL (jobs/8100109002) redirects to an error page (job filled/expired) and does NOT survive. Replaced with a live, fetched Point72 Cloud Operations Engineer listing on builtinnyc.com, which confirms AWS as the platform (EC2, EBS, S3, RDS, IAM, KMS, Terraform) and references on-premise-to-cloud connectivity. No AWS region or facility is named.

CONFIDENCE DOWNGRADE (confirmed -> reported on BOTH sites): The researcher graded both 'confirmed'. That overstates it. The strongest source for the on-prem claim is the Ray *vendor's* (Anyscale) event recap — a credible operator/press-style blog, not a Point72 primary infra disclosure or filing. The cloud claim rests on the same vendor blog plus job ads. Crucially, NO source states WHERE the compute physically sits — both pins are explicitly symbolic at HQ. A map pin whose location is undisclosed and only vendor/job-ad-attested is 'reported', not 'confirmed'. Neither pin represents a documented, geolocatable Point72-owned facility.

SPECIFICS STRIPPED: Removed the unsupported AWS service litany (Glue, EMR, Fargate, Step Functions, Databricks) from the cloud site's capacity_note — no surviving fetched URL backs those; the verified job ad only lists EC2/EBS/S3/RDS/IAM/KMS/Terraform. All power_mw / gpu / cpu fields remain null (correct — no source states any).

COORDINATES: lat/lon 41.0344,-73.5866 sit at/near the Stamford HQ, consistent with city=Stamford, country=USA. coord_precision kept 'symbolic' on both (correct — compute location undisclosed; the coordinate anchors HQ, not the machines).

NOT MAPPED (verified as correctly excluded): A Point72 Market Data / Low-Latency Market Data Engineer job (careers.point72.com, fetched live) references 'co-location market data infrastructure running on RHEL' and 'high frequency trading (HFT) environments within co-locations' — indicating the firm leases execution/market-data colo space, but NO specific facility (Equinix NY4/NY5, Nasdaq Carteret, NYSE Mahwah) is publicly named under Point72, so it cannot be geolocated without inventing a source. Correctly left off the map. Turion (a fund, not compute), Point72 Hyperscale (a PE/VC fund), and the Nscale investment (a portfolio bet, not owned infra) are also correctly excluded.

NET RESULT: 2 surviving symbolic sites at the Stamford HQ, both downgraded confirmed->reported, one URL swapped for a live equivalent, unsupported cloud-service specifics stripped. The finding stands with the Renaissance/Citadel pattern: firm and HQ known, physical compute location deliberately undisclosed — absence of a documented street-level DC is itself the finding.
results.research_notes: Point72 (Steve Cohen, HQ 72 Cummings Point Road, Stamford CT) has NO publicly-documented self-build data center and NO named colocation hall attributable to the firm. Its documented compute footprint is: (1) Cubist Systematic Strategies' on-premise multi-tenant Ray clusters (research compute; physical location undisclosed) and (2) cloud compute via AWS and Anyscale/managed Ray. No MW figures, GPU counts, core counts, or street-level compute facilities appear in the public record for any of these. Both sites are pinned SYMBOLICALLY to the Stamford HQ because the sources establish the existence of the compute but not its physical coordinates. Additional signals worth noting but NOT mapped as distinct located sites: (a) A Point72 'Market Data Support Engineer' job ad references 'co-location market data infrastructure' and 'high frequency trading (HFT) environments within co-locations' (careers.point72.com), indicating the firm leases execution/market-data colo space typical of a multi-manager, but no specific facility (e.g. Equinix NY4/NY5, Nasdaq Carteret, NYSE Mahwah) is publicly named under Point72 — so it cannot be geolocated without inventing a source. (b) 'Turion' is Point72's AI-themed hedge FUND (a trading strategy managed by PM Eric Sanchez), not owned compute. (c) 'Point72 Hyperscale' is a private-equity/venture fund applying AI to portfolio companies (Menlo Park), not a data-center operator. (d) Point72's Nscale investment is a portfolio bet, not owned infrastructure. Recommendation for the map: represent Point72 as a firm whose compute is on-prem-clusters-plus-cloud with location deliberately undisclosed — the ABSENCE of a public street-level DC is itself the finding, matching the Renaissance/Citadel pattern (campus/HQ known, compute location not public).

TASK 1 (fetch every URL): Two of the researcher's three evidence URLs failed — quantitative.com/ returned 404 and financestrategists.com returned 403 — so neither survives as a supporting source. Only virginiabusiness.com fetched, and it confirms QIM is Charlottesville-based and that Woodriff owns the CODE building, but does NOT itself tie QIM's office to CODE. To keep the single HQ pin honest I independently fetched and verified three additional sources: (a) cvilleclimate.org carries a direct QIM statement 'our new office space located within the CODE building' — the strongest QIM-in-CODE link; (b) codebuilding.com confirms the CODE address is 240 W Main St, Charlottesville VA 22902; (c) QIM's LinkedIn lists HQ as '240 W Main Street, Suite 300, Charlottesville, VA 22902' — matching CODE. UVA School of Data Science confirms Woodriff co-founded QIM as a Virginia-based firm. TASK 2 (regrade): confidence stays 'confirmed' — the HQ location is now backed by QIM's own LinkedIn plus a QIM statement plus the building owner's site, all primary/direct. TASK 3 (strip specifics): removed the unverifiable '~40 people' (site 404s); replaced with '~11-50 / ~28' per LinkedIn. No MW/GPU/CPU claimed — none in public record; all null. TASK 4 (location sanity): 240 W Main St is an unambiguous, source-stated full street address in Charlottesville, so coord_precision=exact is justified; lat/lon ~38.0304,-78.4816 sit on that block. TASK 5 (merge/split): single site; no duplicates. This remains an on_prem_hq OFFICE — QIM discloses no dedicated data center, colo, cloud region, grid/EIA MW filing, permit, or PeeringDB facility, and the only GPU-DC candidate (WWT case study) is anonymized and NOT attributable to QIM. Net finding: the absence of any disclosed compute site is itself the finding; the HQ office is the sole publicly documented footprint and must not be upgraded to a compute site without a new primary source.
results.research_notes: QIM (founder Jaffray Woodriff; also Michael Geismar, Greyson Williams; ~$1.1bn AUM; founded 2003) is ML-native by its own description but publishes NO dedicated compute-site disclosure. The only publicly documented physical footprint is its HQ office in the CODE Building, 240 W Main St, Charlottesville VA 22902 — an office, recorded here as on_prem_hq with compute status inferred/undisclosed. No public record ties QIM to a named self-built data center, colocation hall, cloud region (AWS/GCP/Azure), grid-connection/EIA MW filing, planning permit, PeeringDB facility, or contractor announcement. A WWT case study titled "Quant Hedge Fund Builds Software-defined GPU Data Center to Reduce Cloud Dependency" exists but is anonymized ("a quant hedge fund") and CANNOT be attributed to QIM — deliberately excluded to avoid false precision. 1. rentec.com About page (two evidence entries) — both quotes confirmed verbatim on fetch: '50,000 computer cores with over 200 gigabits per second of global connectivity' and 'A research database that grows by more than 40 terabytes a day'. CORRECTIONS (adversarial downgrades):
A. InvestmentNews evidence item REMOVED. The page loads and mentions East Setauket, but the researcher's quoted phrase ('roughly 150 researchers and computer programmers ... 50-acre East Setauket campus') was NOT present on the fetched page, and an independent web search did not corroborate a '50-acre' figure from any citable primary source. Per the rule that a quote must actually appear in the cited page, this evidence entry was dropped and the '50-acre' specific was stripped from capacity_note and coord_basis. This does not drop the site (three strong sources remain).

The researcher's coords (40.945364, -73.085509) match business-directory listings, but Nominatim geocodes the identical address to 40.9307, -73.1106 (~2 km south-west). Route 25A is a long road; no source states exact campus coordinates and no unambiguous parcel confirmation exists, so 'exact' is unjustified. City-level placement in East Setauket is solid and the directory coords are retained as the best approximate point.

NO ADDITIONAL SITES. No named colocation, no cloud region (contrast Citadel on Google Cloud), no MW figure in any grid/permit/EIA filing. results.research_notes:
SCOPE / METHOD: Virtu Financial is a market-maker/HFT firm; its physical footprint is colocation-centric, not self-built data centers. There is NO public evidence of any Virtu-owned/self-built data center, cloud region, or ML/GPU/HPC research campus. This is itself a finding and the deliberate contrast to firms like XTX (self-built Kajaani campus) or Citadel Securities (Google Cloud): Virtu's disclosed compute lives inside third-party exchange-adjacent colocation halls, and no source states MW for any Virtu cage. Virtu's own 10-K frames infrastructure as 'connectivity to data centers, exchanges, markets and liquidity pools' and stresses co-location as 'critical' — i.e., leased colo, not owned compute.

ANCHOR SITE: The single strongest, primary-sourced compute site is Equinix NY5 (Secaucus), where Virtu Americas' POSIT ATS matching engine runs in Virtu's own owned/operated cage (SEC ATS-N filing). The Stockholm STACK/DigiPlex STO01 site is the confirmed European counterpart (operator press release + Virtu CEO quote). IMPORTANT DEDUP: the 'DigiPlex' and 'STACK' press releases are the same October 2021 announcement (DigiPlex rebranded to STACK in 2022) — one facility, not two.

NETWORK INFRA (NOT COUNTED AS COMPUTE SITES): The lead's 'New Line Networks towers (Layer 2)' refers to New Line Networks LLC, the 50/50 microwave-network joint venture of Virtu and Jump Trading. Its FCC-licensed relay masts (e.g. Carteret NJ area — FCC call signs incl. WQWF994/WQWH561 near 40.5867,-74.2428 and 40.5852,-74.2435; and the Aurora IL perch near the CME/CyrusOne data center, Jump's ~31-acre plot bought 2016) are microwave RELAY/transport infrastructure, not compute or data-center halls, so per the compute-footprint goal they are documented here rather than mapped as compute pins. They mark the endpoints (Aurora CME DC and Carteret Nasdaq DC) where Virtu's EXECUTION engines colocate, but no source states Virtu-owned compute or MW at those masts. FCC ULS pages (wireless2.fcc.gov) are JS/anti-bot gated and could not be fetched directly; NLN coordinates above come from FCC-derived tower databases surfaced in search, treat as reported.

CANADA: Virtu operates MatchNow (Canadian ATS) and has a Toronto office (222 Bay St), and Cyxtera/Centersquare runs Toronto-area DCs (Markham YYZ2 / Mississauga YYZ1), but I found no fetched public source explicitly naming which Canadian data center hosts MatchNow's matching engine, so it is deliberately omitted rather than inferred.

CONTACT-PAGE OFFICES (not compute): Austin (2530 Walsh Tarlton Ln — FPGA/hardware-design and low-latency systems team; a hardware lab, not a DC), Chicago (233 S Wacker), Boston, London (Ropemaker Place), Dublin, Paris, Hong Kong, Singapore, Sydney, etc. These are offices, not documented data-center halls, so only the NYC HQ is pinned (symbolically). No MW figure is publicly attributable to any Virtu cage at any site.

I found NO public evidence of: (a) a self-built data centre, (b) any named colocation hall (searched Equinix/Telehouse/Slough/Cambridge providers — nothing links to Cantab), (c) any named cloud region or cloud-provider case study (AWS/GCP/Azure — none found), (d) MW figures from grid/planning/EIA filings, (e) GPU/HPC hardware counts, or (f) a PeeringDB/ASN facility record. I did NOT fabricate any cloud_region symbolic pin because the firm's own materials imply on-prem proprietary infrastructure at HQ rather than a stated cloud-only model; asserting a cloud region would overstate the record. Secondary offices in London and Zurich are administrative/investment teams (GAM group), with no disclosed compute facilities, so they are not mapped as compute sites.

No sites dropped, no confidence downgrades.

URL-BY-URL:
1) flowtraders.com/about/where-we-operate/ — LIVE. HQ address confirmed verbatim: 'Jacob Bontiusplaats 9, Amsterdam 1018 LL, The Netherlands'. Supports Site 3 exact coordinate/precision.
2) flowtraders.com/careers/technology/ — LIVE, but THINNER than researcher claimed. The quote the researcher attributed to it ('Systems, Network, Security and Trading Support are only a few of the focus areas') was NOT found on the page. The actual supported quote is 'Our IT colleagues deploy and maintain the software and hardware infrastructure...'. I replaced the quote on Sites 1 and 3 accordingly. Still supports generic in-house infrastructure; no downgrade needed since each site retains at least one strong firm-primary source.
3) builtinchicago.org/job/trading-systems-engineer/9409938 — content served and clearly a Flow Traders posting, but the page states it was 'removed at 03:21 p.m. (CST) on Monday, Jun 29, 2026' (i.e. technically expired). Content still readable and GCP/GKE + bare-metal quotes verified verbatim. Researcher's paraphrase 'GCP-focused platform-especially GKE' does not appear; corrected to the verbatim 'Serve as the US-based SME for our GCP platform with a strong focus on GKE workloads'. I flagged the expired status in the publisher field. It remains valid corroboration but is the weaker of the two cloud sources.
4) flowtraders.com/careers/job-description/6743078 — LIVE. Verbatim 'Knowledge in Cloud platform environments (AWS and GCP)' and 'Knowledge of Data Center and cabling standards' confirmed. This is the load-bearing firm-primary source for both the cloud (Site 1) and colocation (Site 2) records.
5) flowtraders.com/about/ — LIVE. 'trading hubs in Europe, the U.S. and Asia-Pacific' confirmed verbatim; no specific data center/colocation facility mentioned. Supports Site 2 regional footprint.

INDEPENDENT CHECKS:
- Name-collision check on AS30689 'FLOW-NET' CONFIRMED: it is Flow Jamaica (cable/DSL ISP, Kingston JM, 2-6 Carlton Crescent), NOT Flow Traders the market maker. PeeringDB/ASN triangulation yields nothing usable for this firm. Researcher note #3 is correct.
- Web search for any named Flow Traders self-build / GPU / HPC / ML compute campus / MW figure returned NOTHING. 'FLOW Digital Infrastructure' and 'A-FLOW' (Tokyo/Manila/Laguna data centers) are separate operators — another name collision, correctly excluded.
- No independent tracker (search across Baxtel/DataCenterMap-type results) names any Flow Traders colocation facility. Confirms Site 2 must stay coordinate-less.

CONFIDENCE GRADING: All three kept at 'confirmed' — each is backed by Flow Traders' own primary materials (locations page and/or firm job descriptions). No source states any MW, GPU, or CPU figure, so all such fields remain null (correct). Coordinates: Site 3 'exact' is justified by a documented full street address; Site 1 'symbolic' (no region named); Site 2 has null lat/lon and 'symbolic' precision (unlocatable by design). ABSENCE IS THE FINDING: Flow Traders is an execution + cloud firm with no disclosed self-built compute DC, no ML/HPC/GPU campus, and no public MW — consistent with the execution-firm pattern.
results.research_notes:
Flow Traders is an ETP-focused market maker/HFT firm whose public compute footprint is execution + cloud, with NO self-built compute data center and NO disclosed GPU/HPC/ML training campus, MW figures, or rack counts anywhere in the public record.

KEY FINDINGS:
1) Cloud is explicitly a GCP-focused platform (especially GKE), with AWS as a secondary environment. Stated directly in Flow Traders' own job postings (Trading Systems Engineer, Network Engineer). No specific cloud REGION is ever named, so the cloud site is a symbolic anchor at the Amsterdam HQ. Confidence: confirmed (firm's own materials).

2) The firm runs ultra-low-latency 'bare-metal' / 'on-prem' systems in 'exchange colocation environments' across Europe, the US, and Asia-Pacific, per its own Network Engineer / Trading Systems Engineer postings. BUT the firm never names a single specific colocation facility (no NY4/NY5, LD4, FR2, TY3, Aurora, Carteret, etc. tied to Flow Traders in public sources), and no data-center tracker (Baxtel, DataCenterMap) carries a Flow Traders facility. So this real footprint is captured as an 'undisclosed' entry with no coordinate — it would be inventing precision to pin it.

3) Flow Traders has NO public ASN / PeeringDB record. AS30689 'FLOW-NET' is Flow Jamaica (a Caribbean ISP), a name collision — NOT Flow Traders the market maker. PeeringDB triangulation yields nothing for this firm.

4) The HQ (Jacob Bontiusplaats 9, 1018 LL Amsterdam) is captured as on_prem_hq at exact address precision, but there is no evidence of a dedicated compute/HPC room beyond ordinary office/trading IT.

ABSENCE AS FINDING: no MW, no GPU counts, no CPU core counts, no named self-build, no ML/research compute campus, no grid/EIA/planning filing tied to any Flow Traders compute site. This is a firm that deliberately does not disclose specific facility locations — consistent with the execution-firm pattern (contrast XTX Kajaani). Only the 3 records above are mappable, and only the HQ has a real coordinate; the cloud and colocation entries are symbolic/unlocatable by design.

Additional office addresses were also confirmed on flowtraders.com/about/where-we-operate/ (London 12 Finsbury Square; New York 1114 Ave of the Americas; Chicago 227 W Monroe; Cluj-Napoca 96B Republicii; Milan via San Raffaele 1; Shanghai Jing An Kerry Centre; Singapore Ocean Financial Centre; Hong Kong Hysan Place) but these are offices, not disclosed compute sites, so they are not listed as compute records.

results.reasoning:
Triple-checked the single researcher-proposed site (CoreSite New York campus colocation). Both primary evidence URLs were fetched and both SUPPORT the claim: (1) CoreSite's own IMC customer case study names "IMC Trading" and states its "cutting-edge New York campus offered a highly scalable, network-dense hybrid IT solution" hosting IMC's "development and analytics environments, trading support systems and corporate IT functions" plus native cloud onramps to major public clouds and 60+ networks — a primary operator/colo document, so confidence=confirmed for the fact that IMC is a CoreSite New York campus colocation customer running genuine compute/analytics workloads (distinct from pure exchange-execution colo). (2) CoreSite's New York locations page confirms the campus buildings: NY1 = 32 Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10013; NY2 and NY3 = 2 Emerson Lane, Secaucus, NJ 07094. An independent WebSearch cache reproduced the case-study text verbatim, corroborating it.

KEY CAVEAT preserved: the case study names only the "New York campus" / "New York-area facilities," NOT a specific building. The campus spans Manhattan (NY1) and Secaucus NJ (NY2/NY3), so the Secaucus attribution is INFERRED from campus composition, not source-stated. I therefore keep coord_precision=approximate (not exact) even though 2 Emerson Lane is a documented full address, because we cannot confirm IMC sits in the Secaucus buildings vs the Manhattan NY1 building. I tightened the coordinate to the geocoded 2 Emerson Lane location (~40.7834, -74.0647) since that is where the largest/primary campus buildings are; a geocoding source placed the address there. No MW/kW/cabinet figures are disclosed anywhere, so power_mw stays null (correct). GPU/CPU nulls retained — no source states hardware.

CORRECTION to researcher note: the case study lists IMC's HQ as Chicago (Willis Tower, 233 South Wacker Drive) — the researcher's note framed Amsterdam as HQ. This does not affect the mapped site, which is location-independent of HQ, but I flag the Amsterdam claim as unverified by these sources.

Absence findings judged sound and NOT mapped: (a) cloud usage is mentioned but no named cloud provider/region, so no symbolic cloud_region pin (correctly omitted); (b) Amsterdam HQ has no source naming a specific IMC compute hall; (c) the West Chicago shortwave tower is Tier-2 network/relay, not compute, and its sources (IEEE Spectrum, sniperinmahwah) were NOT independently re-verified here since it is deliberately excluded as out-of-scope for compute; (d) no public record of any IMC self-build/GPU campus was found. One site survives.

CoreSite names only its "New York campus" and "New York-area facilities" — not the specific building. NO SELF-BUILD / GPU CAMPUS FOUND: Unlike the XTX Kajaani anchor case, no public record (DCD, planning filings, grid-connection/EIA, contractor announcements) documents any IMC-owned self-build data center or named GPU/HPC campus. Absence of a named compute facility is itself the finding. power_mw correctly null on all pins (no source states MW). EVIDENCE CHANGES vs researcher output: (1) London — the claimed wintermute.com/company/careers quote ('infrastructure specialists build their advantage in low latency') is NOT on that page (fetched: navigation-only), so that URL was dropped; the CryptoSlate URL returned HTTP 403 and was dropped. results.research_notes: Firm type: crypto market maker (algorithmic MM/OTC liquidity provider), founded 2017, London-HQ'd, ~100+ staff, >$5-15bn daily volume across 60+ exchanges. Infrastructure model is fundamentally different from equities HFT: no evidence of owned/leased venue colocation cages or self-build DCs; trading is API-driven against centralized/decentralized exchanges and is broadly public-cloud based. Job postings confirm DevOps/infrastructure teams split across London, Singapore, and New York (Lever/LinkedIn postings for 'Infrastructure Support Engineer' and 'DevOps Engineer - OTC Trading Platform'), and one posting notes London/Singapore/NY DevOps collaboration and US-hours production stability — consistent with distributed cloud ops, not a single physical compute campus. No MW figure exists in any source (correctly null). London coordinate is exact (documented registered address); Singapore and NY are city-level (no address disclosed). CONFIDENCE on the three pins reflects office existence, NOT compute — none should be presented as a data-centre pin. If this map is strictly compute-facility-only, this firm is effectively found=false / cloud-only-no-named-site; the office pins are included because the schema allows on_prem_hq and the offices are the only physically locatable footprint. Key negative results worth recording: no PeeringDB/ASN record found for Wintermute; AWS web3 tick-to-trade blog series (Parts 1 & 2) does NOT name Wintermute; no colocation-provider (Avelacom/Beeks/Equinix) case study names Wintermute; no planning/permit/grid filing found. Recommend labeling these as offices with a note that compute is cloud/API-based and undisclosed.

CORRECTIONS MADE TO THE RESEARCHER OUTPUT:
1. Site 1 (HQ) — the researcher cited an evidence quote 'AlphaGrep is based in Mumbai, India' attributed to alpha-grep.com/about-alpha-grep/. I fetched that page AND the homepage: neither contains that phrase. The about page only says the firm 'began our journey in a small room in Mumbai' and presents its offices as a global family with no designated HQ; the homepage says '10 offices in Asia, America and Europe.' The quote was unsupported/fabricated, so I removed it. In its place I substituted a genuinely primary, verified source: the firm's own contact page (alpha-grep.com/connect/), which lists the exact Mumbai address 'Parinee Crescenzo Floor 13, Unit 12A-05 G Block, Bandra Kurla Complex Mumbai, Maharashtra 400051' — matching the PeeringDB org record verbatim. The HQ site therefore now has two independent confirming sources (firm contact page + PeeringDB org record) and retains confidence=confirmed and coord_precision=exact, which is justified because Parinee Crescenzo is a single, unambiguous named building whose G-Block BKC coordinates (verified ~19.067-19.069N) contain the stated lat/lon. Capacity note correctly frames this as an OFFICE address, not a disclosed data center — no HPC/DC/MW claimed.

2. Site 2 (Extreme IX Mumbai) — verified via PeeringDB ASN 150560 (IP 103.77.109.69, 10G, Extreme IX Mumbai). I confirmed Extreme IX Mumbai is a distributed exchange spanning ~14 Mumbai PoPs (Equinix MB1/MB2, Web Werks, STT, CtrlS, etc.), so the exact hall genuinely cannot be pinned — city_level is correct, and inferred confidence is appropriate for a PeeringDB interconnect record. Unchanged except tightened wording.

3. Site 3 (Shanghai HPC cluster) — verified the Greenhouse posting is live on AlphaGrep's OWN board (job-boards.greenhouse.io/alphagrepsecurities), title 'HPC Engineer', location Shanghai, describing 'hundreds of accelerators', InfiniBand/RoCEv2, GPUDirect RDMA/Storage, NCCL/CUDA, distributed training. Confirmed the ad names NO facility, GPU model, rack count, or MW. This is a hiring signal, not a disclosed facility — I retained inferred/city_level/office_hpc and hardened the language so no specific building is implied, keeping the job-ad qualifier on the 'hundreds of accelerators' figure. power_mw null everywhere (no source states MW). I also noted the firm's Shanghai office address (Pacific New World T1) exists per the contact page but is NOT tied to the cluster by the ad, so I did not upgrade precision.

NO SITES DROPPED — all three survive with at least one fetched supporting URL (Site 1 now has two). Searches for any self-built data centre, colocation case study, planning/permit, grid filing, contractor announcement, or DCD/DataCenterMap entry naming AlphaGrep returned nothing — confirming the 'absence is the finding' framing. As a global HFT firm AlphaGrep almost certainly leases exchange-colo rack space (NSE/BSE Mumbai and overseas venues) but none is publicly attributed to it by name, so none is mapped. firm set to 'AlphaGrep Securities'.
results.research_notes: AlphaGrep Securities (Mumbai-based prop MM/HFT, founded 2009) keeps its physical compute footprint almost entirely undisclosed. results.research_notes:
Amber Group (crypto market maker, Singapore-HQ; institutional arm Amber International / Amber Premium trades on Nasdaq as AMBR) has a CLOUD-ONLY compute footprint with no named facility. Findings:

WHAT EXISTS (public record):
- Cloud-native, multi-cloud architecture. Independent vendor case studies (API7.ai / APISIX) state services run "across multiple mainstream cloud providers and multi-cloud scenarios." AWS is the only cloud explicitly named (logs migrated off "ELK Stack of AWS"). Other providers referenced generically as "mainstream cloud providers" but not named.
- Uses Cloudflare (CDN/security) and Akamai (network) per Cloudflare customer story and API7 case study — but those are edge/CDN networks, not Amber compute sites, so not mapped.
- HQ: Singapore (Amber Technologies Global Pte Ltd, 182 Cecil St / Frasers Tower, per ACRA registry). Offices also cited in Hong Kong, Japan, Australia, Dubai, EMEA, North America — these are offices, not compute sites, and none is publicly described as an HPC/server room.

WHAT DOES NOT EXIST IN THE PUBLIC RECORD:
- No self-built data centre. No named colocation hall (no Equinix/exchange-colo disclosure). No GPU/ML compute campus. No megawatt figure of any kind. No grid/EIA/planning filing tied to Amber compute.
- No PeeringDB org/ASN for the crypto Amber Group (the 'Amber' entries in PeeringDB are unrelated: AmberIT in Dhaka, Amber Light GmbH in Frankfurt). So no named network facility can be attributed.
- 'AI' activity (amber.ac accelerator, AgentFi, 'Crypto for AI' theme) is an investment/accelerator program, NOT a physical compute buildout — no data-centre or GPU-cluster announcement.

IMPORTANT DISAMBIGUATION: 'Amberdata' (amberdata.io, engineering.amberdata.io) is a SEPARATE company (blockchain data/API provider) and was excluded — its AWS multi-AZ / Redpanda / Databricks details do NOT belong to Amber Group and were deliberately not used as evidence. Likewise 'Amber Infrastructure Group', 'Amber AI & Data Science Solutions GmbH', and the AMBER molecular-dynamics software are unrelated.

CONFIDENCE: The single mapped site is a symbolic cloud_region marker at the Singapore HQ. Coordinate precision is 'symbolic' because the compute is cloud-hosted with no publicly named region — the lat/lon anchors the company, not a data hall. Marked 'confirmed' because the cloud-native/multi-cloud/AWS fact and the HQ address are each backed by primary/vendor-primary sources; the AWS/multi-cloud characterization rests chiefly on the API7 vendor case study (as close to primary as the public record offers for this firm).

Other offices exist (Tokyo, Ho Chi Minh City 2025, Thailand/Malaysia market entry) but are commercial offices with no disclosed compute, so not mapped as compute sites. So the actual machine-room location remains non-public. Symbolic pin at the firm's own stated Hangzhou HQ is the honest placement.
- high-flyer.cn CONFIRMS HQ "14th Floor, Block A, Galaxy International Building, No. 169, Huancheng North Road, Gongshu District, Hangzhou" and describes "Fire-Flyer II is a deep learning platform constructed by High-Flyer AI."

That URL is a 2025-02-11 article about Hangzhou Iron & Steel (杭钢) adapting DeepSeek-R1 — it contains NO Liang Wenfeng colocation-to-self-build quote. I replaced it with the correct source: the "疯狂的幻方" profile (Tencent/QQ News, 2025-02-05, republished from 36Kr), which contains the verbatim quote "几百张卡之前，我们托管在IDC，规模再变大时，托管就没法满足要求了，就开始自建机房" (before hundreds of cards we hosted in IDC; as scale grew, colocation could no longer meet needs, so we began self-building machine rooms). COORDINATES: researcher's proposed 30.3083,120.1636 is not source-stated. INDEPENDENT CORROBORATION SEARCHES: Four searches all reproduced the same Inner Mongolia + Beijing facts, all tracing back to the same 超算地图 article. results.research_notes:
SUMMARY: Lingjun Investment (灵均, Ningbo Lingjun Investment Management Partnership) is a top-tier Beijing quant fund (~US$8.3bn AUM, founded 2014 by Cai Meijie 蔡枚杰). Two distinct public-record compute sites: (1) a self-built supercomputer/AI-compute center in Inner Mongolia (reported established 2022, chosen for cheap >80% green power) and (2) an on-prem server room at the Beijing HQ. The firm's own site advertises 10PB+ storage and 'leading compute, continuous investment.'

KEY GAPS / WHAT IS NOT PUBLIC:
- No city named for the Inner Mongolia site. Inner Mongolia's main compute hubs are Ulanqab (乌兰察布), Hohhot/Helingeer (呼和浩特/和林格尔) national East-Data-West-Compute nodes, but NO source ties Lingjun to a specific one, so coordinates are symbolic (province centroid). Do NOT fake a city.
- No MW figure exists for either site in the public record (power_mw=null for both).
- No GPU model or count is confirmable. Aggregated Chinese coverage claims NVIDIA high-performance GPUs + latest Intel high-frequency CPUs and annual hardware spend of ~2-3亿 RMB (peaking ~10亿 RMB budget 2020-2021), but I could NOT fetch a primary page carrying those exact quotes (the main Zhihu aggregator returned HTTP 403); treated as unverified context, not evidence.
- The 'quant supercomputer map' article exists as near-identical republications on Sina Finance and 36Kr (used as two references); they are the same underlying piece, so this is 'reported', not multi-independent-confirmed, for the Inner Mongolia site.

OTHER LOCATIONS (not included as compute sites): Lingjun also operates a Shanghai office at 华润时代广场 (China Resources Times Square), 张杨路500号, Pudong (~31.238N, 121.526E, exact address on firm's own site). No public evidence of compute/机房 there, so it is omitted as a compute site. Chinese quant funds typically also take exchange colocation (SSE/SZSE/CFFEX/SHFE) for execution, but NO public record ties Lingjun to a named colocation hall, so no execution-colo site is asserted.

DISAMBIGUATION WARNING: 'PAI-Lingjun Intelligent Computing Service' is an unrelated Alibaba Cloud product (Ulanqab/Singapore) — NOT this firm. 'Lingsheng/灵晟' TOP500 supercomputer is also unrelated. Both surfaced repeatedly in searches; excluded.

results.research_notes:
Mingshi Fund (Shanghai Mingshi Private Fund Management, founded Dec 2010 by Prof. Yu Yuan / 袁宇 and Prof. Robert Stambaugh; AUM ~RMB 35bn as of April 2026) is one of the few Chinese quant firms to publicly document a self-built compute program, the '星座计划' (Constellation Plan) supercomputing series run for its Genesis AI Lab (G-Lab, est. 2021).

Physical compute footprint (3 distinct site-states):
1. CURRENT self-build DC — Shanghai Pudong. Supercomputing center relocated here in 2025; phases 仙女座/Andromeda (2022), 英仙座/Perseus (2023), phase 3 loading NVIDIA H200 GPUs; ~15,000 cores (2026), CPU+GPU co-compute, 'cloud+local' hybrid; two-phase build cumulative ~RMB 100M+.
2. HISTORICAL self-build DC — Nanchang, Jiangxi. First-gen Andromeda server room was located here (~8,000 cores as of Dec 2024) before relocation to Shanghai. Same machine program, prior physical location — mapped separately as historical.
3. HQ/on-prem office — 32F West Tower, No.168 Gongping Road, Shanghai (North Bund Raffles City West Tower), Hongkou. Corporate/research HQ and G-Lab team; not confirmed to house the HPC hall.

Caveats and gaps:
- No MW/power figure is disclosed for any site; power_mw left null throughout (per rules).
- No exact street address or named colocation/DC operator is given for either the Nanchang or Pudong compute rooms; both are self-run internal server rooms ('自建高性能机房', run 'by internal staff'). Coordinates are city/district-level (Nanchang city centroid; Pudong district centroid). Only the HQ has an exact building address.
- Hybrid 'cloud+local' architecture is confirmed but NO cloud provider or named cloud region is disclosed — so no separate cloud_region site is created; the cloud element is folded into the Pudong self-build note.
- Confidence: HQ = confirmed (firm's own site). Both compute sites = reported (credible Chinese financial press: China Securities Journal, Sina Finance, 36Kr; plus a firm roadshow/meeting summary). The exact GPU count for H200 phase-3 and the precise Pudong address remain undisclosed.
- The firm's own website (mingshiim.com) served with a TLS chain issue and Zhihu returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch; those pages were read via curl and via search snippets. WWT's anonymous 'Quant Hedge Fund Builds Software-defined GPU Data Center' case study is NOT attributable to Mingshi and was excluded.

No MW/GPU/CPU specifics existed to strip (all null). No duplicates to merge, no records to split, nothing to drop. Overall this is a clean 'absence is the finding' case: ADIA has a substantial quant/AI program but no publicly-documented self-built data centre, colocation hall, or dedicated cloud region — only office/HQ locations, with the compute substrate undisclosed everywhere. The one concrete external-compute signal is ADIA Lab's access to Spain's shared national RES/BSC supercomputing, which is not ADIA-owned infrastructure.
results.research_notes:
KEY FINDING: ADIA has a large quant/AI program but NO publicly-documented self-built data centre, colocation hall, or cloud region of its own. The public record yields only office/HQ locations, and the compute substrate (on-prem vs cloud, GPU counts, MW) is undisclosed everywhere — a genuine "absence is the finding" case. No MW figure exists in any source for ADIA.

Entity distinction (important for the map): (1) ADIA the sovereign wealth fund runs a ~100–125-person Quantitative R&D / Data Science division at its HQ (ADIA Tower, 211 Corniche, Abu Dhabi) — this is the actual quant trading/investing arm named in the task. No compute facility is disclosed for it. (2) ADIA Lab is a separate, ADIA-FUNDED independent research institute (data science / ML / HPC / quantum), HQ'd at Al Khatem Tower (ADGM, Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi) with a planned European HQ in Granada, Spain. ADIA Lab is a research body, not the trading desk, and also operates no named machine of its own — its Spain agreement gives it access to Spain's national RES supercomputing network (shared HPC), the only concrete compute signal found.

Regional context ruling out ADIA-owned supercomputers: the AGBI Middle East supercomputer survey attributes the UAE's HPC systems (Artemis, DOM3) to G42, and other regional machines to Aramco/KAUST/MBZUAI — none to ADIA or ADIA Lab. G42's Stargate UAE (1 GW) is unrelated to ADIA.

Coordinate precision: ADIA Tower is exact (2GIS + firm-confirmed address). Al Khatem Tower is approximate (building known, no source lat/lon). Granada HQ is city-level (building named — Antiguo Hospital de la Salud — but no street address in sources, and still planned/under fit-out).

Sources fetched and used: adia.ae/en/contact, adialab.ae/contact, adialab.ae/aboutadialab, 2gis.ae ADIA Tower listing, top1000funds.com, agbi.com supercomputers survey, lamoncloa.gob.es (Spanish govt primary), granada.org (city council), ahoragranada.com, computerweekly.com quantum feature.
